member.name: T:QPlayer.Audio.QAudioFileReader
member.summary:
A smart audio reader, which picks an appropriate decoder for the given file path, opens the file, creates any needed stream converters to get a 32bit float sample stream, and buffers audio intelligently.
The methods are entirely lock-free and intended to be called from a dedicated audio thread, while all the other methods are intended to be called from the main thread. This is with the exception of the methods which can be called from a separate audio buffering thread (this is done automatically, if an is passed in the constructor). Please be aware that the threading model for this class is quite complicated (there are potentially 3+ threads with contention over this object), most methods are not reentrant unless explicitly mentioned (or trivial).
The lifecycle of this object is described as follows:
MainThread: QAudioFileReader::ctor() \--> registers the new object with the AudioBufferingDispatcher ... SamplePosition.set() --> repositions the stream when needed, this blocks until the current FillBuffer operation is complete
AudioBufferingDispatcher worker: while true: if audioFile.NeedsFilling: FillBuffer() --> fills one of the internal audio buffers if needed
AudioThread: Read() --> gets as many samples as are available (or requested) from an audio buffer, never blocks, but may not return as many samples as requested

member.name: P:QPlayer.Audio.QAudioFileReader.NumSamples
member.summary: The number of samples in this stream.

member.name: P:QPlayer.Audio.QAudioFileReader.SamplePosition
member.summary: The position in samples within the input stream.

member.summary: The starting position in samples within the input stream. Setting this clears the buffer containing the start of the file used for seamless looping.

member.summary: Gets how many samples are still available in the current buffer.

member.summary: Gets whether this reader has an empty buffer waiting to be filled with .

member.summary: Gets whether this reader has an empty start buffer waiting to be filled .

member.name: M:QPlayer.Audio.QAudioFileReader.ReleaseBuffers
member.summary: Releases the internal buffers used by this reader so they can be reused elsewhere. Call this method when the audio file is not being played.

member.name: M:QPlayer.Audio.QAudioFileReader.Read(System.Single[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Fill the specified buffer with 32 bit floating point samples. This method is not reentrant.
member.remarks: This method might not entirely fill the requested of samples if they're not available yet. Furthermore, it may return -1 if no samples are currently available but the end of the stream hasn't been reached yet. A return value of 0 always indicates that the stream has ended.
member.param: The buffer to fill.
The offset into to start writing.
The number of samples requested.
When , syncronously waits for the buffer to fill before returning.
member.returns: The nunber of samples read from the source.

member.name: M:QPlayer.Audio.QAudioFileReader.FillBuffer
member.summary: Fills the internal buffer of this reader with samples. Must be called sufficiently frequently to avoid starving the audio file reader. This method can be called from external threads, but is not reentrant.

member.name: M:QPlayer.Audio.QAudioFileReader.FillStartBuffer
member.summary: Fills the special 'start' buffer of this reader with samples. Must be called sufficiently frequently to avoid starving the audio file reader. This method can be called from external threads, but is not reentrant.

member.name: M:QPlayer.Audio.QAudioFileReader.ReadExactly(System.Single[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Repeatedly reads from the until either the requested number of samples are read, or the sample provider returns 0 samples.
member.param: The buffer to read into.
The offset into the buffer to start writing.
How many samples to read.
Whether the returned 0 samples on the last read.
member.returns: The number of samples read.

member.summary: Repositions the internal safely, and warms it up by reading into the junk buffer.

member.name: M:QPlayer.Audio.QAudioFileReader.SeekReader(System.Int64)
member.summary: Intelligently seeks this to the given sample position, reusing existing buffers if possible. Automatically invalidates the sample buffer if needed. The underlying is not repositioned until the next read.
member.param: The position in samples to seek to.

member.returns: The aligned position in samples.

member.name: T:QPlayer.Audio.VectorExtensions
member.summary: This class provides a number of vectorised maths routines, which are optimised for X86 SIMD instructions.

member.name: M:QPlayer.Audio.VectorExtensions.PrintSignal(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Single},System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Pretty prints an audio signal as a waveform. Based on https://github.com/sudara/melatonin_audio_sparklines/blob/main/melatonin_audio_sparklines.h
The signal is represented as a series of dashes, with a few special characters indicated specific values:
0 -- a single 0 value 0(23) -- 23 consecutive zeros x -- a zero-crossing N -- NaN I -- +-Inf S -- subnormal values E -- outside of the -1 to 1 range
For interleaved multi-channel signals, each chanel is displayed on it's own line. If a signal is prepended with a + then every sample is >= 0.
member.param: The signal to display.
The number of interleaved channels.
Whether the signal should be collapsed to skip repeated values.
Whether the signal should be normalized.
The maximum number of characters to return.

member.summary: Multiplies the pairs of values in a by the values in b, mutating a with the result.

member.name: M:QPlayer.Audio.VectorExtensions.ApplyPan(System.Span{System.Single},System.Single)
member.summary:
Pans a span of interleaved stereo samples left or right.
Lout = Lin * 1 - max(0,pan) Rout = Rin * 1 + min(0,pan)

member.name: F:QPlayer.Models.MSCCommands.Go

member.summary: This is the same as a Pause command in grandMA2. This can be followed by a cue number.

member.summary: This will "un-plause" a cue. If a specific cue has been paused, then the cue number needs to be specified with this command.

member.summary: This can be used to perform a Goto with a specific fade time. It needs both the time and the cue number - in that order.

member.summary: Set can be used to set the position of faders. It needs the fader number and page followed by the position.

member.name: F:QPlayer.Models.MSCCommands.Fire
member.summary: This can be used to trigger macros. The macro number needs to follow the command. Only macro 1 to 255 can be triggered.

member.name: T:QPlayer.Utilities.StringDict`1
member.summary: A fast string-keyed dictionary. This is very similar in implementation to the .NET generic dictionary, but it has a few specific optimisations which can be useful in some cases. Notably, it can by accessed by string key or by of which can save unnecessary string allocations. It also exposes methods to get values by reference which can save performance. And it uses a slightly faster string hashing algorithm.
member.typeparam: The type of values stored in this dictionary.

member.name: F:QPlayer.Utilities.StringDict`1.buckets
member.summary: Stores references to dictionary items, index by key hashcode. Stores a 1-based index into the other arrays.

member.name: F:QPlayer.Utilities.StringDict`1.hashcodes
member.summary: Stores the complete hashcode of an item pointed to by buckets.

member.name: F:QPlayer.Utilities.StringDict`1.links
member.summary: Stores the index of the next item in the same bucket as the current item pointed to by buckets. 1-based index!

member.name: F:QPlayer.Utilities.StringDict`1.keys
member.summary: Stores the key of an item pointed to by buckets.

member.name: F:QPlayer.Utilities.StringDict`1.values
member.summary: Stores the value of an item pointed to by buckets.

member.name: M:QPlayer.Utilities.StringDict`1.Optimize(System.Single,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Optimises the dictionary for red performance and memory efficiency.
member.param: The load factor to target. Smaller values result in faster reads at the cost of more memory used. A good value is between 1 and 3.
Whether to compact all the entries in the slots. Saves a bit of space if many items have been removed from the dictionary.

member.name: M:QPlayer.Utilities.StringDict`1.Remove(System.String,`0@)
member.summary: Removes an item with the specified key from the dictionary, and returns the removed value.
member.param: The key of the element to remove.
The value associated with the key which was removed.
member.returns: if the item was successfully removed from the dictionary.

member.summary: Gets the value associated with the given key. The reference is only valid so long as the dictionary is not mutated.

member.returns: A reference to the value in the dictionary if it exists, or a null reference.

member.returns: An of all the values which match the given keys.

member.name: T:QPlayer.ViewModels.BindableViewModel`1
member.summary:
The base class for any view model which can be bound onto a separate model instance.
This class implements all the necessary logic to automatically synchronise changes from a view model to a model. The behaviour of the automatic bindings can be controlled using the various model attribtues (, , ). Implementing classes should be annotated with and where applicable.
member.remarks: The default automatic binding behaviour is to bind all properties with a public getter and setter on the ViewModel to all public fields with matching (case insensitive) names on the Model.
member.typeparam: The type of the model to bind to.

member.name: M:QPlayer.ViewModels.BindableViewModel`1.Bind(`0)
member.summary:
Binds this to the specified model instance. Automatically propagates proeprties changes from this object to the model, but not the other way around.
When using the source generator, this method is automatically implemented so long as the deriving class defines at least one reactive property (see ).
member.param: The model to bind to, or to unbind.

member.name: M:QPlayer.ViewModels.BindableViewModel`1.SyncFromModel
member.summary:
Copies all bound property values on this instance from the bound model.
When using the source generator, this method is automatically implemented so long as the deriving class defines at least one reactive property (see ).

member.name: M:QPlayer.ViewModels.BindableViewModel`1.SyncToModel
member.summary:
Copies all bound property values on this instance to the bound model.
When using the source generator, this method is automatically implemented so long as the deriving class defines at least one reactive property (see ).

member.name: M:QPlayer.ViewModels.BindableViewModel`1.OnSyncFromModel
member.summary: When using a source generator, this method is automatically implemented and should be called in .

member.name: M:QPlayer.ViewModels.BindableViewModel`1.OnSyncToModel
member.summary: When using a source generator, this method is automatically implemented and should be called in .

member.summary: Moves the given cue in the cue stack to the specified index.
member.param: The cue to move in the cue stack.
The index within the cue stack to move the cue to.
Whether the moved cue should be reselected.
Optionally, the new QID to assign to the cue once it's moved. Otherwise, this method will choose a new QID itself (recommended).
Whether an undo item should be recorded.
member.returns: if the cue was moved successfully.

member.name: M:QPlayer.ViewModels.MainViewModel.MoveCue(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Nullable{System.Decimal},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Moves the given cue in the cue stack to the specified index.
member.remarks:
The insertion index behaves a little bit odd compared to regular insertion behaviour. To illustrate:
srcInd: [0] [1] [2] [3] [4] dstInd: 0 1 2 3 4 5
The cue is always inserted at an index 'between' two cues, so if we want to move q1 to before q0, we would pick index 0, but to move it after q2, we would pick index 3. Effectively, dstInd 1 and 2 don't move the cue.
member.param: The cue to move in the cue stack.
The index within the cue stack to move the cue to.
Whether the moved cue should be reselected.
Optionally, the new QID to assign to the cue once it's moved. Otherwise, this method will choose a new QID itself (recommended).
Whether an undo item should be recorded.
member.returns: if the cue was moved successfully.

member.name: M:QPlayer.ViewModels.MainViewModel.CreateCueNoInsert(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Decimal},QPlayer.ViewModels.CueViewModel)

member.name: M:QPlayer.ViewModels.MainViewModel.ChooseQID(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Generates a QID at the given insertion point in the cue stack, renumbering cues if needed.

member.name: M:QPlayer.ViewModels.UndoManager.ClearUndoStack
member.summary: Clears the undo and redo history. Necessary after drastic changes which would invalidate the undo history, this also allows the GC to reclaim objects being kept alive by the undo history.

member.name: T:QPlayer.SourceGenerator.ReactiveAttribute
member.summary:
Automatically generates a property for the annotated field which automatically raised PropertyChanged notifications when set.
This attribute can also be used on existing properties to generate a reactive property for that property. To this, the annotated property must either be partial (in which case a backing field will also be generated), or the parameter must be specified.
member.remarks: If a custom property name is not specified, the generated property will take the name of field with the first letter capitalised, or prefixed with an underscore. IE: int exampleProp -> int ExampleProp, and int OtherExample -> int _OtherExample
member.param: The name of the reactive property to generate.

member.name: M:QPlayer.SourceGenerator.ReactiveAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary:
Automatically generates a property for the annotated field which automatically raised PropertyChanged notifications when set.
This attribute can also be used on existing properties to generate a reactive property for that property. To this, the annotated property must either be partial (in which case a backing field will also be generated), or the parameter must be specified.
member.remarks: If a custom property name is not specified, the generated property will take the name of field with the first letter capitalised, or prefixed with an underscore. IE: int exampleProp -> int ExampleProp, and int OtherExample -> int _OtherExample
member.param: The name of the reactive property to generate.

member.name: T:QPlayer.SourceGenerator.CachedNotification
member.summary: When this property is updated use a cached instance of .

member.name: T:QPlayer.SourceGenerator.SkipEqualityCheckAttribute
member.summary:
By default, when this property is updated, the old value is compared to the new value using the == operator, and only if the value are different is the setter and property change notification invoked. This attribute skips this check, this can be useful if no == is available or if this equality comparison would be very expensive.
Requires a on this same property.

member.name: T:QPlayer.SourceGenerator.ChangesPropAttribute
member.summary:
When this property is updated, a property change notification for the specified property is also raised.
Requires a on this same property.
member.param: The name of the property to generate a change notifcation for.

member.name: M:QPlayer.SourceGenerator.ChangesPropAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary:
When this property is updated, a property change notification for the specified property is also raised.
Requires a on this same property.
member.param: The name of the property to generate a change notifcation for.

member.name: T:QPlayer.SourceGenerator.IconAttribute
member.summary:
Specifies the icon for a given cue type.
The icon is defined as a in a (in xaml). The plugin loader automatically creates an instance of the given resource dictionary and binds it to the app if one doesn't already exist. The parameter should refer to a given x:Key="name" attribute on the in xaml.

The type of the resource dictionary which contains the given icon.
